behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git rename detection plus CLI parsing/aggregation. A mock/stub diff can hide real failures where git reports rename vs add+delete depending on invocation/options (e.g., similarity detection). This behavior is not about one pure function only; it depends on subprocess output semantics from an actual repo state.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI via subprocess, and assert report shows 1 rename and 0 paired add/delete for that path.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: The key risk is real subprocess/encoding behavior for binary diffs. In-process fixtures often miss decode/exit/format edge cases from actual git output. MOCK-HIDE applies: stubbing text diff output can pass while real binary diff handling crashes.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp git repo, add/modify a binary file, run the CLI subprocess, assert zero crash (exit success) and binary file reflected in category counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: This hinges on real git behavior for unborn HEAD/no-commit repos (commands, stderr, exit codes). A mock can hide exactly the failure mode this behavior targets.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo with no commits, run CLI subprocess, assert successful exit and an empty structured report.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly validates correctness against actual repository history, which requires real git object/index/worktree semantics and subprocess interaction. Rejecting lower levels: Integration is disqualified because behavior text requires matching the real repository history, not a mocked history feed.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a real repo history with known adds/deletes/renames/modifies across commits, run CLI subprocess for the target range, and assert counts exactly match expected history-derived totals.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: This is a git index vs working-tree parity check, a real state boundary across OS filesystem + git index behavior. Mocked module tests can miss real differences in how staged/unstaged diffs are emitted.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create equivalent staged and unstaged change scenarios, run CLI subprocess for each, and assert byte-identical structured stdout.
